G0001.gloss: the first letter of the alphabet
G0001.strongs_def: of Hebrew origin; the first letter of the alphabet; figuratively, only (from its use as a numeral) the first: . Often used (usually an, before a vowel) also in composition (as a contraction from ἄνευ ) in the sense of privation; so, in many words, beginning with this letter; occasionally in the sense of union (as a contraction of ἅμα ).

G3561.strongs_def: feminine of a compound of νέος and μήν (as noun by implication, of ἡμέρα ); the festival of new moon: .

G3562.strongs_def: adverb from a comparative of the accusative case of νοῦς and ἔχω ; in a mind-having way, i.e. prudently: .

G3564.strongs_def: probably contracted for a compound of νύμφη and δῶρον ; nymph-given (i.e. -born); Nymphas, a Christian: .

G3568.strongs_def: a primary particle of present time; "now" (as adverb of date, a transition or emphasis); also as noun or adjective present or immediate: . See also τανῦν , νυνί .

G3599.strongs_def: perhaps from the base of ἐσθίω ; a "tooth": .

G3606.strongs_def: from ὅς with the directive enclitic of source; from which place or source or cause (adverb or conjunction): .

G3608.gloss: neuter of a presumed derivative of ὀθόνη
G3608.strongs_def: neuter of a presumed derivative of ὀθόνη ; a linen bandage: .

G3610.strongs_def: from οἰκέω ; a fellow resident, i.e. menial domestic: .

G3613.strongs_def: neuter of a presumed derivative of οἰκέω (equivalent to οἴκημα ); a residence (literally or figuratively): .

G3655.strongs_def: of uncertain affinity; a thunder storm: .

G3656.strongs_def: from ὅμιλος ; to be in company with, i.e. (by implication) to converse: .

G3661.strongs_def: adverb from a compound of the base of ὁμοῦ and θυμός ; unanimously: .

G3678.strongs_def: neuter of a presumed derivative of ὄνος ; a little ass: .

G3715.strongs_def: from ὀρέγομαι ; excitement of the mind, i.e. longing after: .

G3718.strongs_def: from a compound of ὀρθός and the base of τομώτερος , to make a straight cut, i.e. (figuratively) to dissect (expound) correctly (the divine message): .

G5405.strongs_def: probably the same as φοῖνιξ ; Phœnix, a place in Crete: .

G5409.strongs_def: from φόρος ; to have a burden, i.e. (by analogy) to wear as clothing or a constant accompaniment: .

G5412.strongs_def: from φόρτος ; to load up (properly, as a vessel or animal), i.e. (figuratively) to overburden with ceremony (or spiritual anxiety): .

G5414.strongs_def: from φέρω ; something carried, i.e. the cargo of a ship: .

G5415.strongs_def: of Latin origin; "fortunate"; Fortunatus , a Christian: .

G5416.gloss: neuter of a derivative from the base of φραγελλόω
G5416.strongs_def: neuter of a derivative from the base of φραγελλόω ; a whip, i.e. Roman lash as a public punishment: .

G5417.gloss: to whip, i.e. lash as a public punishment
G5417.strongs_def: from a presumed equivalent of the Latin flagellum ; to whip, i.e. lash as a public punishment: .

G5418.strongs_def: from φράσσω ; a fence, or inclosing barrier (literally or figuratively): .

G5419.gloss: probably akin to φράσσω through the idea of defining
G5419.strongs_def: probably akin to φράσσω through the idea of defining; to indicate (by word or act), i.e. (specially), to expound: .

G5422.gloss: to be a mind-misleader, i.e. delude
G5422.strongs_def: from φρεναπάτης ; to be a mind-misleader, i.e. delude: .

G5426.strongs_def: from φρήν ; to exercise the mind, i.e. entertain or have a sentiment or opinion; by implication, to be (mentally) disposed (more or less earnestly in a certain direction); intensively, to interest oneself in (with concern or obedience): .

G5428.strongs_def: from φρονέω ; mental action or activity, i.e. intellectual or moral insight: .

G5431.strongs_def: from a derivative of φρήν ; to exercise thought, i.e. be anxious: .

G5434.strongs_def: neuter of a presumed derivative of phrugo (to roast or parch; akin to the base of φλόξ ); something desiccated, i.e. a dry twig: .

G5435.strongs_def: probably of foreign origin; Phrygia, a region of Asia Minor: .

G5438.strongs_def: from φυλάσσω ; a guarding or (concretely, guard), the act, the person; figuratively, the place, the condition, or (specially), the time (as a division of day or night), literally or figuratively: .

G5440.strongs_def: neuter of a derivative of φυλάσσω ; a guard-case, i.e. "phylactery" for wearing slips of Scripture texts: .

G5443.strongs_def: from φύω (compare φύλλον ); an offshoot, i.e. race or clan: .

G5454.strongs_def: of uncertain derivative; a burrow or lurking-place: .

G5455.strongs_def: from φωνή ; to emit a sound (animal, human or instrumental); by implication, to address in words or by name, also in imitation: .

G5456.strongs_def: probably akin to φαίνω through the idea of disclosure; a tone (articulate, bestial or artificial); by implication, an address (for any purpose), saying or language: .

G5459.strongs_def: from φῶς and φέρω ; light-bearing ("phosphorus"), i.e. (specially), the morning-star (figuratively): .

G5461.kjv_usage: enlighten, illuminate, (bring to, give) light, make to see

G5487.strongs_def: from χάρις ; to grace, i.e. indue with special honor: .

G5566.strongs_def: of Latin origin; the north-west wind: .

G5567.strongs_def: probably strengthened from psao (to rub or touch the surface; compare ψώχω ); to twitch or twang, i.e. to play on a stringed instrument (celebrate the divine worship with music and accompanying odes): .

G5568.gloss: a set piece of music, i.e. a sacred ode
G5568.strongs_def: from ψάλλω ; a set piece of music, i.e. a sacred ode (accompanied with the voice, harp or other instrument; a "psalm"); collectively, the book of the Psalms: . Compare ᾠδή .

G5569.gloss: a spurious brother, i.e. pretended associate
G5569.strongs_def: from ψευδής and ἀδελφός ; a spurious brother, i.e. pretended associate: .

G5570.gloss: a spurious apostle, i.e. pretended pracher
G5570.strongs_def: from ψευδής and ἀπόστολος ; a spurious apostle, i.e. pretended pracher: .

G5571.strongs_def: from ψεύδομαι ; untrue, i.e. erroneous, deceitful, wicked: .

G5572.gloss: a spurious teacher, i.e. propagator of erroneous Christian doctrine
G5572.strongs_def: from ψευδής and διδάσκαλος ; a spurious teacher, i.e. propagator of erroneous Christian doctrine: .

G5573.gloss: mendacious, i.e. promulgating erroneous Christian doctrine
G5573.strongs_def: from ψευδής and λέγω ; mendacious, i.e. promulgating erroneous Christian doctrine: .

G5574.gloss: middle voice of an apparently primary verb
G5574.strongs_def: middle voice of an apparently primary verb; to utter an untruth or attempt to deceive by falsehood: .

G5575.gloss: a spurious witness, i.e. bearer of untrue testimony
G5575.strongs_def: from ψευδής and a kindred form of μάρτυς ; a spurious witness, i.e. bearer of untrue testimony: .

G5576.gloss: to be an untrue testifier, i.e. offer falsehood in evidence
G5576.strongs_def: from ψευδομάρτυρ ; to be an untrue testifier, i.e. offer falsehood in evidence: .

G5578.gloss: a spurious prophet, i.e. pretended foreteller or religious impostor
G5578.strongs_def: from ψευδής and προφήτης ; a spurious prophet, i.e. pretended foreteller or religious impostor: .

G5580.strongs_def: from ψευδής and Χριστός ; a spurious Messiah: .

G5581.strongs_def: from ψευδής and ὄνομα ; untruly named: .

G5582.strongs_def: from ψεύδομαι ; a fabrication, i.e. falsehood: .

G5584.gloss: to manipulate, i.e. verify by contact
G5584.strongs_def: from the base of ψάλλω (compare ψῆφος ); to manipulate, i.e. verify by contact; figuratively, to search for: .

G5585.gloss: to use pebbles in enumeration, i.e
G5585.strongs_def: from ψῆφος ; to use pebbles in enumeration, i.e. (generally) to compute: .

G5586.strongs_def: from the same as ψηλαφάω ; a pebble (as worn smooth by handling), i.e. (by implication, of use as a counter or ballot) a verdict (of acquittal) or ticket (of admission); a vote: .

G5587.strongs_def: from a derivative of psithos (a whisper; by implication, a slander; probably akin to ψεύδομαι ); whispering, i.e. secret detraction: .

G5588.strongs_def: from the same as ψιθυρισμός ; a secret calumniator: .

G5589.gloss: diminutive from a derivative of the base of ψάλλω
G5589.strongs_def: diminutive from a derivative of the base of ψάλλω (meaning a crumb); a little bit or morsel: .

G5590.strongs_def: from ψύχω ; breath, i.e. (by implication) spirit, abstractly or concretely (the animal sentient principle only; thus distinguished on the one hand from πνεῦμα , which is the rational and immortal soul; and on the other from ζωή , which is mere vitality, even of plants: these terms thus exactly correspond respectively to the Hebrew נָ֫פֶשׁ , ר֫וּחַ and חָי ): .
G5590.kjv_usage: heart (+ -ily), life, mind, soul, + us, + you

G5591.strongs_def: from ψυχή ; sensitive, i.e. animate (in distinction on the one hand from πνευματικός , which is the higher or renovated nature; and on the other from φυσικός , which is the lower or bestial nature): .

G5594.strongs_def: a primary verb; to breathe (voluntarily but gently, thus differing on the one hand from πνέω , which denotes properly a forcible respiration; and on the other from the base of ἀήρ , which refers properly to an inanimate breeze), i.e. (by implication, of reduction of temperature by evaporation) to chill (figuratively): .

G5595.strongs_def: from the base of ψωμίον ; to supply with bits, i.e. (generally) to nourish: .

G5596.gloss: diminutive from a derivative of the base of ψώχω
G5596.strongs_def: diminutive from a derivative of the base of ψώχω ; a crumb or morsel (as if rubbed off), i.e. a mouthful: .

G5597.gloss: prolongation from the same base as ψάλλω
G5597.strongs_def: prolongation from the same base as ψάλλω ; to triturate, i.e. (by analogy) to rub out (kernels from husks with the fingers or hand): .

G5598.gloss: the last letter of the Greek alphabet, i.e
G5598.strongs_def: the last letter of the Greek alphabet, i.e. (figuratively) the finality: .

G5599.strongs_def: a primary interjection; as a sign of the vocative case, O; as a note of exclamation, oh: .

G5600.gloss: , including the oblique forms, as well as es
G5600.strongs_def: , including the oblique forms, as well as es ; e ; etc. the subjunctive of εἰμί ; (may, might, can, could, would, should, must, etc.; also with εἰ and its comparative, as well as with other particles) be: .
G5600.kjv_usage: + appear, are, (may, might, should) be, X have, is, + pass the flower of her age, should stand, were

G5601.strongs_def: of Hebrew origin ( עוֹבֵד ); Obed, an Israelite: .

G5602.gloss: in this same spot, i.e. here or hither
G5602.strongs_def: from an adverb form of ὅδε ; in this same spot, i.e. here or hither: .

G5603.strongs_def: from ᾄδω ; a chant or "ode" (the general term for any words sung; while ὕμνος denotes especially a religious metrical composition, and ψαλμός still more specially, a Hebrew cantillation): .

G5604.strongs_def: akin to ὀδύνη ; a pang or throe, especially of childbirth: .

G5605.gloss: to experience the pains of parturition
G5605.strongs_def: from ὠδίν ; to experience the pains of parturition (literally or figuratively): .

G5606.gloss: perhaps from the alternate of φέρω
G5606.strongs_def: perhaps from the alternate of φέρω ; the shoulder (as that on which burdens are borne): .

G5607.strongs_def: , including the feminine ousa ; and the neuter on present participle of εἰμί ; being: .

G5608.gloss: middle voice from an apparently primary onos
G5608.strongs_def: middle voice from an apparently primary onos (a sum or price); to purchase (synonymous with the earlier πίμπρημι ): .

G5610.strongs_def: apparently a primary word; an "hour" (literally or figuratively): .
G5610.kjv_usage: day, hour, instant, season, X short, (even-)tide, (high) time

G5611.gloss: belonging to the right hour or season
G5611.strongs_def: from ὥρα ; belonging to the right hour or season (timely), i.e. (by implication) flourishing (beauteous (figuratively)): .

G5612.gloss: middle voice of an apparently primary verb
G5612.strongs_def: middle voice of an apparently primary verb; to "roar": .

G5613.gloss: probably adverb of comparative from ὅς
G5613.strongs_def: probably adverb of comparative from ὅς ; which how, i.e. in that manner (very variously used, as follows): .
G5613.kjv_usage: about, after (that), (according) as (it had been, it were), as soon (as), even as (like), for, how (greatly), like (as, unto), since, so (that), that, to wit, unto, when(-soever), while, X with all speed

G5614.strongs_def: of Hebrew origin ( יָשַׁע and נָא ); oh save!; hosanna (i.e. hoshia-na), an exclamation of adoration: .

G5615.gloss: as thus, i.e. in the same way
G5615.strongs_def: from ὡς and an adverb from αὐτός ; as thus, i.e. in the same way: .
G5615.kjv_usage: even so, likewise, after the same (in like) manner

G5616.strongs_def: from ὡς and εἰ ; as if: .
G5616.kjv_usage: about, as (it had been, it were), like (as)

G5617.strongs_def: of Hebrew origin ( הוֹשֵׁ֫עַ ); Hosee (i.e. Hoshea), an Israelite: .

G5618.strongs_def: from ὡς and περ ; just as, i.e. exactly like: .

G5619.gloss: just as if, i.e. as it were
G5619.strongs_def: from ὥσπερ and εἰ ; just as if, i.e. as it were: .

G5620.strongs_def: from ὡς and τέ ; so too, i.e. thus therefore (in various relations of consecution, as follow): .
G5620.kjv_usage: (insomuch) as, so that (then), (insomuch) that, therefore, to, wherefore

G5621.strongs_def: diminutive of οὖς ; an earlet, i.e. one of the ears, or perhaps the lobe of the ear: .

G5622.strongs_def: from a derivative of the base of ὠφέλιμος ; usefulness, i.e. benefit: .

G5623.gloss: to be useful, i.e. to benefit
G5623.strongs_def: from the same as ὠφέλεια ; to be useful, i.e. to benefit: .

G5624.strongs_def: from a form of ὄφελος ; helpful or serviceable, i.e. advantageous: .
